title: Deep Suite Long-Run Handoff Payload
description: Payload shape for cross-plugin long-run handoff artifacts (e.g., deep-work Phase 5 → deep-evolve outer loop, or evolve epoch → deep-work resume). MUST be wrapped in the M3 artifact-envelope (schemas/artifact-envelope.schema.json) with envelope.schema = { name: 'handoff', version: '1.0' } and envelope.artifact_kind = 'handoff'. The envelope's run_id/parent_run_id forms the cross-plugin trace; this payload describes the handoff intent and continuation brief.

^x-.description: Forward-compat extension namespace. Reserved-and-ignored — never breaks old validators.

schema_version.description: Payload schema version. Locked to 1.0 for v1; forward-compat via x-* patternProperties. Breaking change → publish handoff-v2.schema.json.

handoff_kind.description: Canonical handoff scenarios. 'phase-5-to-evolve' = deep-work Phase 5 Integrate hands off to deep-evolve outer loop. 'evolve-to-deep-work' = deep-evolve epoch end hands continuation back. 'slice-to-slice' = mid-session slice transition (rare; usually receipts cover this). 'session-resume' = handoff serialized for >24h dormant session resume. 'custom' = user-defined; producer SHOULD set a more specific kind in x-handoff-subkind.

producer.description: Originating plugin (kebab-case; matches sidecar plugin keys + envelope.producer).

session_id.description: Originating session identifier (e.g., deep-work session timestamp slug).

phase.description: Phase or epoch name at handoff time (e.g., 'integrate', 'epoch-3-complete'). Producer-defined vocabulary.

completed_at.description: RFC 3339 timestamp when the originating work reached the handoff point.

producer.description: Target plugin expected to consume this handoff.

intent.description: Short label of what the receiver should do (e.g., 'performance-optimization', 'security-hardening', 'test-coverage-improvement'). Drives consumer's inner-loop seed prompt.

scope_hint.description: Optional scope narrowing (e.g., 'auth-middleware', 'src/lib/cache/*'). Consumer SHOULD respect this as a soft boundary.

summary.description: Human-readable summary of what the originating run accomplished. Should fit in a single paragraph; the receiver agent reads this verbatim.

key_artifacts.description: Concrete artifacts the receiver should read first. Path is repo-relative or, for wiki content, `<wiki_root>`-prefixed. The optional run_id chains to the M3 envelope of the referenced artifact when applicable.

run_id.description: Optional. M3 envelope run_id of the referenced artifact, for trace reconstruction.

open_questions.description: Unresolved decisions the originating run flagged. The receiver should surface these to the user before deep work, not silently make a call.

completed_actions.description: Major actions the originating run completed (e.g., 'merged PR #123', 'committed slice 4/7 GREEN', 'wiki page jwt-auth updated'). Helps the receiver avoid re-doing work and recognize what context already exists in artifacts.

next_action_brief.description: One-paragraph brief that becomes the receiver's seed prompt. Must contain the WHY (motivation), the CURRENT STATE (baseline metric / status), and the TARGET (numeric where possible). Example: 'Optimize token verification — current p99=180ms, target <50ms; consumer code in src/auth/jwt.ts; expensive verify call is on every middleware hop.'

context_window_state.description: Optional. Records how the originating run compacted context before serializing the handoff. Consumers may use this to decide whether a full reset is needed (e.g., handoff older than 24h + key_artifacts referenced).

compaction_strategy.description: What was preserved in producer memory at handoff time. 'key-artifacts-only' = paths in key_artifacts[] are the only re-readable signal. 'receipt-only' = a session-receipt artifact summarizes everything. 'summary-only' = the summary field alone reconstructs context. 'full-history' = full conversation log retained (rare for long-run). 'custom' producer-specific (set x-compaction-strategy-detail).

preserved_artifact_paths.description: Concrete artifact paths the producer kept in memory through compaction. Receiver SHOULD read these before initiating work.
